ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Continued To Apr-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Sisneros (Practice Administrator) in Propria Persona.
Austin Jackson for Steven Laub.
Jerome Blum appeared by telephone for Miller Law Group (Creditor).

Mr. Blum stated that he is not satisfied with the accounting that was provided by Mr. Sisneros as is it makes no indication of his Partner's cases, dates or recovery amounts, and requested the Court order an accounting from Mr. Sisneros on all of the case he distributed to all of the Attorneys. Mr. Sisneros stated that he only had five cases, that Mr. Graziani is now his Partner, but was not at the time Mr. Laub passed away, that he has no access to that information, that he has asked for information and was told by Mr. Graziani that he will not provide any information. The Court noted that twenty appearances have been made on this matter. The Court signed an order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than April 2, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by April 4, 2018. The Second Amended First Accounting, etc. and Petition for Order Compelling Practice Administrator to Disclose the Assets, etc. are continued to and the Parties are to return on April 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2017 Continued To Jan-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jerome Blum appeared by telephone for Miller Law Group (Creditor).
Roger Cisneros Practice Administrator in Propria Persona.
Austin Jackson for Steven Laub.

Mr. Blum stated that he is seeking to be paid for the judgment amount of $34,000.00. Mr. Cisneros stated that there is no intent for Miller Law to get paid, that there is no money in the account, that there are Client funds in the Client Trust account and that he can not use those funds to pay a Creditor. Mr. Blum stated that he has significant issues with the accounting and the Administrator's failure to comply with the Court order with regard to providing specific information about the cases (20 to 30), that he received a list, but nothing makes sense, made reference to the meet and confer letters (what occurred, how much money was collected, did Arnold Laub share in those cases), stated that his position is that it is inappropriate for the Court to approve the accounting without having complied with the Court order and that Mr. Cisneros and his Partner had a one third interest in some of those cases. Mr. Cisneros stated that he provided everything in 2015, that there were no written contracts or liens on those cases that were assigned to him, that there were no contracts with the Attorney they were assigned to, that some settled, but no money came to Laub and that he has case numbers. Mr. Cisneros explained how the Laub Office operated, stated that other Attorneys were involved with from the beginning, that there is no way to enforce payment because there were no contracts or liens, that those cases were assigned prior to Mr. Laub's passing, that he had no written agreements for the seven cases, that the oral agreement was 1/3 to him and 2/3 to Laub, that he did not pay it because Mr. Laub did not do anything and was not paying for any case costs, that the Laub Trust was not interested, that the original contracts were breached and that he does not think they are enforceable. The Court stated that Mr. Graciani and Mr. Cisneros should account for those cases they took over, that Mr. Cisneros has a conflict and that he is taking the position of a creditor. The Court orders an accounting of those cases which is due by January 22, 2018. The Petition for Order Compelling Practice Administrator to Disclose Assets, etc. and the Second Amended First Accounting and Report, etc. are continued to January 29, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Original bank statements are required by the hearing date. Mr. Cisneros stated that there were three Client Trust accounts, that two of the accounts were escheated to the State, that the one at East West Bank has $7,900.00, that they are identified Clients and that he is having a hard time locating them.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Z. Grimaldi. Not reported.

Mr. Sisneros stated that the first accounting was drafted by Mr. Hatter. He said that information to complete the accouting was in the hands of an accountant, but work on it had stopped over a dispute as to who was to pay the accountant. Mr. Barulich said that his client is trying to get the estate buttoned up, but that the accounting practices of the law firm were abysmal. He stated that the trustee has a fiduciary duty to not waste money on a wasting asset. He requested that the Court issue an order that the trust has no interest in the law firm. The Court stated that he could not do that merely based upon a request in court, and that Mr. Barulich would need to file a separate petition eeking such relief. Mr. Barulich stated that he would need 30 days to do that, and requested that the hearing be continued.
